evidence: Berger diagonal reference operator and Hopf twist are already represented in the complete-operator package.
limitations: This is an action-origin uniqueness input, not a new prediction.

evidence: v1.2/v1.2B/v1.2C derive the omega functional from the symbolic parent boundary scaffold under BHSM axioms.
limitations: Global uniqueness of arbitrary actions is not claimed beyond the listed BHSM axioms.

evidence: Chiral projector channel excludes generated mirror candidates in the scaffold chain.
limitations: Full H_T still depends on separate mirror/index closure.
ingredients.role: weak chirality projector and mirror guard

evidence: Sector-labeled formal kernel and sector-coupling block are the only allowed lepton/up/down mixing package.
limitations: Sector coupling commutator control remains a downstream H_T dependency.

evidence: Formal kernel coordinates are sector-labeled and not coordinate-first.
PSD/profile and lift terms are represented by existing operator package terms.
ingredients.role: heat lift and positive semidefinite profile sector

evidence: v2.11/v2.12 close mixed coefficient and bundle-curvature formula gaps with BUNDLE_CONNECTION_SEPARATION_WITH_TOPOGRAPHIC_REPRESENTATION.
limitations: This is a BHSM axiom-level uniqueness input, not empirical fitting.
ingredients.role: topographic representation of mixed connection and R_bundle

evidence: Free mixed coefficients are forbidden because they would alter local SM bundle dynamics.
forced_operator_terms: forbid independent free mixed operator terms
limitations: Uniqueness is under the BHSM local-separation axiom.
ingredients.role: local Standard Model bundle dynamics remain locally unchanged

evidence: Alternative terms that open mirror leakage are theorem-failing rather than allowed deformations.
limitations: Full mirror theorem is downstream; this uniqueness audit only forbids adding mirror-opening terms.

evidence: The old coordinate-first protected block is excluded by formal sector-label alignment.
limitations: This prevents an artifact from selecting the operator package.
The inventory closes the action-origin ingredient list under current BHSM axioms.
It does not by itself prove the full H_T theorem or authorize final paper preparation.
title: BHSM v2.13 Operator Action Ingredient Inventory
